title: Peking Opera heritage short
content: Peking Opera intangible cultural heritage short films, cinematic sense, oriental aesthetics, warmth and restraint. In the backstage of traditional opera troupes and handicraft workshops, the old master quietly makes Peking Opera headgear, organizes costumes, and outlines face masks. The details of the hands are delicate, and the silk thread, beading, paint and embroidery patterns are very textured. A young apprentice watches carefully next to him, then carefully takes the tools and completes the small steps under the guidance of the old master. The old master straightens his head and organizes his clothes, like a craft and an emotion gently handed over to him. Finally, the young man is neatly dressed and stands by the stage about to play, with a dim light illuminating the costumes and side faces, and the old master watches quietly behind him. The overall atmosphere is quiet, affectionate, and has a sense of inheritance. There are few subtitles, and appropriate lines are interspersed.
